name: Chicago-Style Pizza
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Shredded low fat Mozzarella cheese, divided
2 medium Tomatoes, cut into thin wedges
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine flour, yeast, and salt in a mixing bowl. Stir in water and olive oil to form a ball.
2. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured board and knead for 1 minute.
3. Shape the dough into a ball and place it in a lightly greased bowl. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 30 to 40 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, combine soy sauce, Italian herbs, garlic, mushrooms, tomatoes, and bell pepper in a bowl.
5. Punch down the dough and divide into 6 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a circle about 5 inches in diameter.
6. Place circles on lightly oiled baking sheets, building up edges slightly. Brush with half of the soy sauce mixture.
7. Sprinkle with 1 cup cheese. Divide vegetables between pizzas and brush with remaining soy sauce mixture.
8. Top with remaining cheese.
9. Bake at 450 F for 15 to 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
